mechanism: Red light activates phytochrome B photoreceptor; Pfr form accumulates in light.
Phytochrome C is activated by red light; Ma5 locus encodes SbPhyC.
Dawn light entrains the morning oscillator: CCA1 expression peaks at dawn and is light-gated. Conservation across Arabidopsis and grasses (rice OsCCA1).
Dawn light activates LHY morning expression alongside CCA1 in the core oscillator. Light entrainment is a canonical clock input.
SbFKF1/SbFFL contains a LOV domain that forms a covalent adduct with FMN upon blue-light illumination, activating the F-box protein to recruit substrates via SCF ubiquitin ligase.
Long-day light evening phase coincides with SbPRR37 evening mRNA peak via PhyB; in SD the evening peak is eliminated because the phase occurs in darkness.
LD induces evening peak of SbGhd7 via PhyB; SD eliminates this peak (darkness).
PhyB (Ma3) is required for evening-light activation of SbPRR37 in long days; loss of PhyB eliminates the evening expression peak of SbPRR37.
PhyB activates SbGhd7 expression through evening-phase light signaling in long days.
PhyB represses SbCN15 (SbFT1 = Hd3a-subclade florigen) via a mechanism that is PRR37/Ghd7-independent and photoperiod-independent. Loss of PhyB causes ~60-fold upregulation of SbCN15.
PhyB/PhyC mediate SbCO protein destabilisation in LD daylight hours, reducing CO activity (opposite of Arabidopsis); in SD this is released.

PhyB inactivates ELF3 post-translationally; in rice, OsPhyB inactivates OsELF3 through post-translational modifications in response to light, thereby attenuating Evening Complex repression on Ghd7/PRR37 in LD.
Phytochrome C (Ma5) acts together with PhyB to delay flowering in LD through activation of floral repressors; PhyC-light signaling contributes to PRR37 activation.
PhyC contributes to evening-light signaling that activates Ghd7 in LD, parallel to PhyB.
Conserved circadian oscillator: morning-phased CCA1 binds EE elements in the TOC1 promoter and represses its transcription.
Evening-phased TOC1 represses CCA1 transcription, completing the core oscillator feedback loop.
LHY and CCA1 act redundantly to repress TOC1 in the morning.
Morning-phased CCA1/LHY represses GI to restrict GI expression to the afternoon/evening peak.

Morning-phased CCA1/LHY MYB TFs repress evening-phased genes including PRR37; canonical across Arabidopsis and grass oscillators. Conservation inferred for sorghum from the clock-gated bimodal SbPRR37 expression (Murphy 2011).
ELF4 and LUX physically associate with ELF3 to form the Evening Complex; ELF3 serves as docking scaffold. Without ELF4/LUX, ELF3 loses repressive activity on clock output.
Evening Complex subunit ELF3 represses morning/evening PRR genes; inferred in sorghum from rice OsELF3-1 ortholog, which negatively regulates OsPRR1/37/73/95 (Zhao 2012).
Evening Complex subunit ELF3 represses Ghd7; in rice, OsELF3 suppresses Ghd7 expression, accelerating flowering under LD. Mechanism conserved in sorghum Evening Complex.
Evening Complex DNA-binding subunit LUX targets promoters of morning/evening PRR genes; rice OsLUX recruits OsELF3-1 and OsELF4s to repress Hd1 and Ghd7. Mechanism inferred for sorghum LUX ortholog.
LUX binds the Ghd7 promoter as part of the Evening Complex; rice evidence (OsLUX recruits ELF3/ELF4 to repress Ghd7).
SbGhd7 binds the SbELF3 promoter at the TGAATG motif and directly represses its transcription (Tadesse 2024 ChIP-seq + DAP-seq).
SbGhd7 directly represses SbFKF1 (ChIP-seq target, TGAATG binding motif).
GI is required for normal diurnal SbPRR37 expression; sbgi-ems1 loses 2-5 fold PRR37 abundance across key time points.
GI-FKF1 complex stabilizes CO and promotes CO transcription at 12 h after dawn, coincident with SbGI peak expression.
GI activates Ehd1 expression, mainly under SD at ZT12 (SD peak strongly attenuated in sbgi).
Blue-light-activated FKF1 forms a complex with GI that stabilizes CO protein and promotes CO transcription at late afternoon.
Ma2 locus (Sobic.002G302700, SMYD lysine methyltransferase) increases SbPRR37 expression amplitude in LD. Loss of Ma2 (ma2 = L141*) reduces PRR37 peak amplitude, advancing flowering.
Ma2 activates SbCO expression in LD; loss of Ma2 reduces both SbCO expression peaks.
SbID1 establishes the capacity for SbCO expression; sbid1-2 mutants lose wildtype SbCO waveform across all time points.
SbID1 required for proper SbPRR37 diurnal expression; sbid1-2 abolishes PRR37 peaks.
SbID1 required for proper SbGhd7 diurnal expression; primary Ghd7 peak at ZT16 is absent in sbid1-2.
SbID1 (maize ID1 / rice Ehd2 ortholog) activates SbEhd1 expression. In rice, Ehd2 up-regulates Ehd1 under both SD and LD; ehd2 mutants show 145-365 day flowering delays.
SbID1 required for SbCN8/SbFT8 florigen expression peaks.
SbID1 required for SbCN12/SbFT10 florigen expression.

In LD, SbPRR37 upregulates SbCO expression at dawn (opposite to Arabidopsis). Differential SbCO expression between LD and SD requires PRR37. NOTE: this activation edge is paired with the PRR37-CO co-repressor action on SbCN8/12 (E025 below) — the net effect of PRR37 in LD is floral repression via the CO-Ehd1-FT axis.
SbPRR37 represses SbEhd1 expression in LD, preventing the induction of florigens.
SbPRR37 suppresses SbFT1/SbCN15 florigen expression in LD via repression of SbEhd1 and/or direct repression in co-repressor complex with SbCO.
SbPRR37 represses SbCN8 (SbFT8) florigen expression in LD. Casto/Murphy 2019 show PRR37 and CO act as co-repressors on SbCN8/SbCN12 in LD.
SbPRR37 represses SbCN12 (SbFT10) florigen in LD.
SbGhd7 directly binds the SbEhd1 promoter (TGAATG motif) and represses its transcription. Direct ChIP-seq/DAP-seq target.
SbGhd7 represses SbFT1 (SbCN15) florigen in LD via repression of SbEhd1 and direct binding.
SbGhd7 represses SbCN8 (SbFT8) florigen in LD, both directly and via SbEhd1 repression.
SbGhd7 directly binds the SbFT10 promoter and represses its transcription. Tadesse 2024 shows SbFT10 as the major directly-bound florigen target.
SbCO activates SbEhd1 expression ~25-fold in SbCO-2 backgrounds vs sbco-null.
SbCO activates SbCN8 (SbFT8) florigen expression ~10-fold (canonical CO → FT activation, day-length regulated at protein level).
SbCO strongly activates SbCN12 (SbFT10) florigen ~100-fold.
SbCO mildly activates SbCN15 (SbFT1) florigen (effect small compared to other florigens).
SbEhd1 activates SbFT1 directly; SbEhd1 OE promotes SbFT1 promoter activity.
SbEhd1 directly activates SbFT8 (SbCN8) florigen promoter.
SbEhd1 directly activates SbFT10 (SbCN12) florigen promoter.
SbFT1 protein physically interacts with SbFD1 to form the florigen activation complex (FAC) in the shoot apical meristem.
SbFT1 binds sorghum 14-3-3 scaffold protein; all three florigens bind 14-3-3.
SbFT8 binds Sb14-3-3 scaffold protein (florigen activation complex).
SbFT10 binds Sb14-3-3 scaffold protein (florigen activation complex).
Florigen activation complex (SbFT1-FD-14-3-3) activates SbSOC1 (SbMADS31) expression in the shoot apical meristem.
Florigen SbFT8 activates SbSOC1 (SbMADS31) in the meristem.
Florigen SbFT10 activates SbSOC1 in the meristem (major florigen, directly repressed by Ghd7).
Conserved SOC1 → LFY activation in the floral transition.
SOC1 activates AP1 in the floral transition; SbMADS7 is the sorghum AP1/FUL clade MADS.
LFY directly activates AP1 transcription; mutual reinforcement in floral meristem identity.
FD bZIP TF acts with the florigen-FD-14-3-3 FAC complex to activate AP1 at the shoot apical meristem, triggering floral meristem identity. Canonical module conserved from Arabidopsis FD.
sbi-miR156h cleaves SbSBP19 transcripts via complementary site in 3' UTR.
sbi-miR156h targets SbSBP4 for cleavage.
SPL/SBP TFs (e.g. SbSBP19) directly bind MIR172a promoter and activate its transcription (age-pathway module).
sbi-miR172a cleaves SbAP2 / Sobic.001G036800 (rap2.7-ortholog) transcripts, relieving AP2-mediated repression of flowering.
Maize rap2.7 ortholog represses ZmFT (ZCN8); sorghum AP2/Sobic.001G036800 likely represses SbFT8/SbFT10 in similar fashion.
SPL/SBP TFs directly activate SOC1 expression at the meristem (age-pathway convergence with photoperiod pathway). Conserved from Arabidopsis SPL9 → SOC1 and maize ZmSPL targets.
SbFT1 florigen promotes the vegetative-to-reproductive transition in the shoot apical meristem, reducing days to flowering.
SbFT8 florigen accelerates flowering transition.
SbFT10 is the major florigen in sorghum (directly repressed by Ghd7); promotes flowering transition.

SbFT12 (Sb06g012260, FlrAvgD1, panicoid-specific PEBP) is a floral suppressor; loss-of-function confers photoperiod-insensitive early flowering. Reduction-of-function at CAAT-box deletion in 5'UTR drove temperate adaptation.
SbAP2 (Sobic.001G036800, rap2.7-ortholog) represses flowering; loss (via miR172) relieves repression.
SbSOC1 (SbMADS31) activates LFY/AP1 in the shoot apical meristem, triggering floral meristem identity.
SbAP1 (SbMADS7) commits the meristem to floral identity and triggers flowering.
Sb14-3-3 is a required scaffold of the florigen activation complex (binds all three florigens). Loss of 14-3-3 function breaks FAC assembly and delays flowering.
Under inductive SD, early-morning peak of GA20/GA1 coincides with floral induction, hastening flowering. GA promotes flowering in grasses via DELLA destabilisation and FT activation.

Direct integrative effect of SbID1 on flowering time via activation of SbCO, SbEhd1, SbFT8 (SbCN8), and SbFT10 (SbCN12). In the algebraic framework the three-florigen cascade is diluted by geometric-mean activation and rescued by bounded-inverse inhibition when PRR37/GHD7 co-drop; adding a direct short-cut edge captures the empirical 170-day flowering delay of sbid1-2 that is not faithfully transmitted through the multistep cascade. This is a modeling short-cut per REFINEMENT_AGENT Section 1.5 Step C, not a novel biological claim.
